nameMeanings.name: Morgan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh (especially Glamorgan): from the medieval Welsh personal name Morgan, Morgant (Old Welsh Morcant, of uncertain etymology).
Irish: importation of the Welsh surname (see 1 above), to which has been assimilated more than one Gaelic surname, notably Ó Muireagáin (see Merrigan ).
Scottish: probably from a Gaelic personal name cognate with Welsh Morcant.

variants.name: Merrigan
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Muireagáin ‘descendant of Muireagán’ a personal name derived from muir ‘sea’. Compare Morgan , with which this name has been confused.

variants.name: Morgans
meanings: Welsh (especially Glamorgan): variant of Morgan , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Jeven
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, originally a nickname from Anglo-Norman French jovene ‘young’ (Latin iuvenis). Use as a given name has probably been influenced by its similarity in sound to Evan .

nameMeanings.name: Leuan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Jeven Leuan Morgan was born in 1404, in Tredegar, Monmouthshire, Wales, his father, Llewelyn Ap Morgan, was 54 and his mother, Jenet verch Dafydd, was 40. He had at least 1 son with Denise Elizabeth verch Thomas. He died in 1492, in his hometown, at the age of 88.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Jeven Leuan Morgan est né en 1404, à Tredegar, Monmouthshire, Galles, son père, Llewelyn Ap Morgan, avait 54 ans et sa mère, Jenet verch Dafydd, avait 40 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Denise Elizabeth verch Thomas. Il est décédé en 1492, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 88 ans.
